reasoning: On Wikipedia, FIDE rankings are updated from the official published list on the 1st of each month. Pragg's official March 2025 rating was 2758, which will serve as the Wikipedia baseline on 2025-03-30 (since the April list is unpublished until April 1). A 1% increase over 2758 requires a rating of ~2786. Even if the platform strictly uses the 2746 live rating quoted in the prompt as the baseline, he would need to reach ~2774. For the first two resolution dates in April, Wikipedia will reflect the April 1 official FIDE list. Pragg recently lost points at the Prague Masters in early March, bringing his live rating to ~2746, which will lock in as his official April rating. This makes an increase over either 2758 or 2746 mathematically impossible for April. For later dates, a jump of 28 to 40 points is formidably difficult at the Super GM level (where he is already top 10), requiring a historic win streak over a long timeframe. Thus, odds remain very low through mid-2025 and only stretch higher toward 2026, granting enough time for a possible breakout.
